Both DW and CW are natural whey substrates characterized only by bulk analytical parameters (total nitrogen, total phosphorus, proteins), not reconstitutable/BiGG-mappable defined formulations; rejected as a natural-substrate feeding regime. Values documented for the record. Grown aerobically.
evidence: Realkalized fed-batch fermentation II was fed with a mixture of a 400 g/L concentrated lactose and CW medium. The CW medium (6.3% dry weight) contained (in g/L): lactose, 48.1; total nitrogen, 1.05; total phosphorus, 0.43; proteins, 5.0; pH, 4.7. Aerobic (30 C, 200 rpm, 0.5 L/h aeration).

name: mineral medium with acetate (electron donor) and N2O (electron acceptor); full composition in Conthe et al. 2018b/c (PMC6153640)
note: SOURCE PAPER DOES NOT CONTAIN THE MEDIUM COMPOSITION. PMC6153640 (Conthe et al. 2018, on N2O-respiring communities) explicitly defers the full mineral-medium recipe to Conthe et al. 2018b (Environ. Microbiol. Rep.) and 2018c (ISME J). Accessible facts confirmed FROM this paper: (1) carbon source / electron donor = acetate (sodium acetate); (2) sole electron acceptor = N2O (100% N2O diluted in Argon) -> cultivation is ANAEROBIC, not aerobic; (3) N source = ammonium; (4) 20 C, pH 7, chemostat dilution rate 0.026/h. No numeric concentrations for phosphate buffer, KH2PO4/K2HPO4, MgSO4, CaCl2, trace elements, or vitamins are given anywhere in the text, tables, or supplements. Cannot confirm or correct the full formulation from this paper without fabricating; recipe must be pulled from Conthe et al. 2018b/c. IMPORTANT CORRECTIONS regardless of verdict: current record has aerobic=true and oxygen=facultative and lists an O2 exchange (EX_o2_e) -- this is WRONG; the system respires N2O under Argon and is anaerobic (no O2). The auto-extractor's O2 component should be removed and oxygen set to anaerobic.
evidence: "The reactor set-up, operation, sampling, and medium composition are described in detail in Conthe et al. (2018b, c)." The paper itself gives no mineral-salt recipe; it only states "100% pure N2O gas diluted in Argon gas was fed to the chemostat at a total flow rate of 200 ml/min" (electron acceptor, anaerobic), acetate/sodium acetate as electron donor and carbon source, and ammonium (NH4+) monitored as N source, at 20 C, pH 7.

name: RMS (rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate) (PMC6381291)
note: RMS is a real chemically-defined rhizobia minimal medium with succinate as carbon source, but PMC6381291 does NOT provide its composition — it cites the formulation to Broughton et al., 1986. No salts, N source, buffer, vitamins, or trace elements are given in this paper, so the auto-extracted 2-component record (O2 + succinate only) cannot be verified or completed from this source. Oxygen corrected to aerobic based on 190 rpm shaken culture at 27°C (current record said 'facultative'). Full formulation requires manual sourcing from Broughton et al., 1986 (J Gen Microbiol). Carbon source succinate is correct; carbon amount not stated in this paper.
evidence: "NGR234 and derivative strains were grown at 27°C in/on rhizobia minimal medium supplemented with succinate (RMS) (Broughton et al., 1986) or tryptone yeast (TY) medium (Beringer, 1974)"; cultures grown at 27°C with 190 rpm shaking (aerobic).

name: Aquil-tricho (natural oligotrophic South China Sea surface seawater, 0.22 um-filtered, microwave-sterilized) (PMC9640675)
note: REJECTED as a defined medium: the base is NATURAL, undefined oligotrophic seawater (unknown ionic/organic composition), so it is not a chemically defined, BiGG-mappable formulation. Only the enrichments (phosphate, unspecified vitamins, EDTA-buffered trace metals) are defined; major salts are inherited from natural seawater and never specified. No organic carbon source (CO2-fixing) and no added N source (diazotroph). Oxygen corrected to aerobic (light-grown aerobic marine cyanobacterium), not 'facultative' as auto-extracted.
evidence: Base: natural seawater, '0.22 um-filtered and microwave-sterilized oligotrophic South China Sea surface water', 'enriched with various concentrations of chelexed and filter-sterilized NaH2PO4' and 'filter-sterilized vitamins and trace metals buffered with 20 uM EDTA'. Organism Trichodesmium erythraeum IMS101 is diazotrophic (N2-fixing, no added N source) and photoautotrophic (CO2). Chemostat reservoir 1.2 uM NaH2PO4; steady-state SRP 12.4-16.3 nM.

name: Natural seawater medium (Santa Barbara Channel, aged, 0.2um filtered, autoclaved) + trace elements (PMC10092583)

name: Cgxii (PMC10158823)
note: This paper does NOT define CGXII; it only references it in Table 2 as another study's medium (partial: 20 g/L yeast extract; 5 g/L urea). The auto-extracted record is fabricated/mis-derived: D-glucose 82 g/L, acetate 24 g/L, potassium 24 g/L, and biotin 0.2 mg/L do not appear in the paper, and the '20 g/L yeast extract' was mis-split into ammonium (20 g/L) + sulfate (20 g/L). evidence: Paper title: 'System metabolic engineering of Escherichia coli W for the production of 2-ketoisovalerate using unconventional feedstock.' The auto-extracted record invented D-glucose 81 g/L (not in the paper) and dropped the two defining complex ingredients (yeast extract, corn steep liquor). The cited salts (0.4 g/L KCl -> K+ + Cl-, 0.1 g/L MgSO4 -> Mg2+ + SO4, 5 g/L (NH4)2SO4 -> NH4 + SO4) were extracted correctly but belong to the other study. Because it contains undefined complex ingredients (yeast extract + corn steep liquor) and is not attributable to this paper, it should be removed or re-sourced. name: Bioconversion in 0.1 M potassium phosphate buffer + lactose (growth/induction in rich or modified M9 media) (PMC10850783)
note: This record does NOT represent a defined growth medium. It is a resting-cell BIOCONVERSION buffer (0.1 M potassium phosphate + a sugar substrate such as 10 mM lactose) in which cells do not grow (no nitrogen source, no defined salt/vitamin package). The auto-extractor incorrectly applied an 'M9 base expansion', injecting M9 salts (Na, NH4, Mg, SO4, Ca, Cl) and a full trace-element set (Fe2/Fe3, Mn, Zn, Cu, Co, Mo, Ni) that the paper never specifies for this buffer -- these are fabricated and must not be published. The paper's actual growth medium was 'modified M9' (M9 salts + 33 mM thiamine + 0.1% casamino acids + 0.2% glycerol) or TB, which is a distinct entity from this bioconversion buffer. Bioconversions are O2-dependent P450 oxidations, hence aerobic. Recommend rejecting/retiring this record; if a growth medium is desired, create a separate 'modified M9' record instead.
evidence: Methods: bioconversions were carried out with whole/resting cells resuspended in "0.1 M potassium phosphate (Kpi) buffer pH 7.5" ("0.1 M Kpi buffer pH 7.0" for alkanes) supplemented with sugar substrate, e.g. "10 mM lactose". Growth/induction was done separately, aerobically (37 C, 220 rpm shaking), in TB or modified M9 = "M9 salt + 33 mM thiamine, 0.1% (w/v) casamino acids, 0.2% (v/v) glycerol".

name: M9 minimal medium + 0.1% (w/v) glucose (PMC3782435)

Glucose is not among the 0.1% (w/v) carbon supplements; it is only mentioned in passing batch-culture comparisons (Fig. S3) with no defined M9 + 0.1% glucose formulation.

name: defined (chemically defined) medium (PMC8614739)
note: PMC8614739 (error-propagation/regression study) does NOT describe the medium composition; it references DeLisa et al. 1999 (ref 28) for the E. coli defined medium and Paul et al. 2019 (ref 30) for the CHO chemically defined medium. Therefore the composition cannot be confirmed or corrected from this paper. The auto-extracted components glycerol and lactose are NOT stated in this article and appear fabricated/misattributed; ammonium (12.5% NH3) as nitrogen source and aerobic conditions (DO>30%, O2 supplementation, 37 C, pH 6.8) ARE supported. To verify the full defined-medium formulation, the primary source DeLisa et al. 1999 must be consulted. Marked not_found because this specific paper does not enumerate the medium.
evidence: The paper states the E. coli was 'cultivated on defined media given in [28]' (referring to DeLisa et al., 1999) and the CHO cells in 'a chemically defined medium; see [30]' (Paul et al., 2019); the medium formulation itself is not enumerated in this article. The only medium-related specifics given are operational: 37 C, pH 6.8 maintained by adding 12.5% NH3 (used as primary nitrogen source), aerobic with dissolved oxygen kept above 30% and pure-oxygen supplementation.

name: Defined 2,3-BD production medium (Jung et al., 2012) with 40 g/L glucose (PMC8531500)
note: PMC8531500 (Enterobacter aerogenes IAM1183, 2,3-butanediol production) describes a single medium, and it is semi-defined (contains 5 g/L yeast extract and 10 g/L Casamino acids). There is NO fully chemically defined variant in the paper, so a 'defined 2,3-BD production medium' record is not supported by the source. This record is effectively a mislabeled duplicate of the semi-defined medium record and should be removed or relabeled; the correct full composition is captured under the semi-defined id. Micro-aerobic cultivation; E. aerogenes is a facultative anaerobe.
evidence: The paper describes only ONE medium: "(g/L) 6.8 Na2HPO4, 3 KH2PO4, 0.75 KCl, 0.28 Na2SO4, 5.35 (NH4)2SO4, 34.2 ZnCl2, 0.26 MgSO4·7H2O, 0.42 citric acid, 10 Casamino acids, 5 yeast extract". Because it contains yeast extract and Casamino acids, this medium is SEMI-defined, not defined.

name: ATCC 1161 medium (PMC7745072)
note: Paper PMC7745072 (Mesoplasma florum L1) was fetched and read successfully. It only NAMES 'ATCC 1161 medium' as the culture medium and reports incubation temperature (optimum 34 C, no growth >36 C); it does NOT reproduce the medium's composition (no salts, carbon/N source, buffer, vitamins, trace elements, serum or amounts are given in the article). Therefore the medium is not_found within this source. The current auto-extracted record (heart infusion broth + horse serum + phenol red + 4% sucrose + 200 U/ml penicillin G + a yeast-extract complex decomposition) is NOT supported by any text in this paper and appears imported from external ATCC/GrowthDB documentation; its full formulation could not be verified against the paper and should not be treated as paper-confirmed. Oxygen not stated in the paper; left as facultative (Mesoplasma/Mollicutes are typically facultatively anaerobic) rather than asserting aerobic. Full ATCC 1161 formulation, if desired, must be sourced from ATCC medium documentation, not this article, during manual curation.
evidence: The optimal growth temperature of the type strain M. florum L1 was therefore evaluated in ATCC 1161 medium by measuring the doubling time at different incubation temperatures.
